message:
docs: lead with shims approach, add shims page, tone down to experimental

- README: lead with module shims as key value prop, mark experimental
- index.mdx: humble framing, experimental badge
- shims.mdx: new dedicated page with full API coverage docs
- astro.config: add Module Shims to sidebar after Getting Started

files.patch:
@@ -1,174 +1,149 @@
 # nodemode
 
-Map Node.js primitives to Cloudflare Workers + R2 + Durable Objects + Containers.
+> **Experimental** — research prototype exploring whether Node.js module shims can make existing npm packages work on Cloudflare Workers. Not production-ready.
 
-Run tools that need `fs`, `child_process`, and shell — things impossible on vanilla Workers — on Cloudflare's edge.
+An experiment in shimming `node:fs` and `node:child_process` for Cloudflare Workers, inspired by how [vinext](https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext) shims `next/*` imports.
 
-## Conformance: 235 tests passing
+The idea: if wrangler aliases `node:fs` to an R2+SQLite-backed implementation, libraries that `import fs from "node:fs"` might just work — without a Container, at $0 cost.
 
-Proves nodemode can support popular Node.js tools that require shell + filesystem:
+## How it works
 
-| Tool | Stars | What it needs | Tests | Status |
-|------|-------|---------------|-------|--------|
-| **opencode** (AI agent) | — | fs read/write, exec, refactoring, configs, monorepo | 61 | PASS |
-| **simple-git** | 3.5k+ | .git/ fs, refs, tags, remotes, merge conflicts, large files | 36 | PASS |
-| **zx** (Google) | 44k+ | pipes, chains, exit codes, conditionals, batch ops | 35 | PASS |
-| **create-next-app / create-vite / degit** | 130k+ / 70k+ / 7k+ | mkdir, write templates, npm install | 19 | PASS |
-| **lint-staged / nodemon / esbuild** | 13k+ / 26k+ / — | fs.watch, spawn linters, read imports, write bundles | 17 | PASS |
-| **Unit tests** | — | All fs ops, 24 builtins, shell parsing, validation, container | 67 | PASS |
-
-## Mapping
-
-| Node.js | Cloudflare |
-|---------|------------|
-| `fs` | R2 (file content) + DO SQLite (directory index & cache) |
-| `child_process` | 24 built-in command emulators + Containers for heavy workloads |
-| `stdio` | WebSocket with DO SQLite terminal buffer |
-| `process` | DO instance state (env, cwd, argv) |
-
-## Quick Start
+```jsonc
+// wrangler.jsonc — alias Node.js modules to nodemode shims
+{
+  "alias": {
+    "fs": "nodemode/shims/fs",
+    "child_process": "nodemode/shims/child_process"
+  }
+}
+```
 
 ```ts
-// wrangler.toml
+// worker/index.ts
 import { Workspace, createHandler } from "nodemode";
-
 export { Workspace };
 export default { fetch: createHandler() };
 ```
 
-```ts
-// Client SDK (browser, Node.js, Workers)
-import { NodeMode } from "nodemode/client";
+Then libraries that use `node:fs` or `node:child_process` resolve to nodemode's shims:
 
-const nm = new NodeMode("https://my-worker.workers.dev", "my-workspace");
-await nm.init({ owner: "me", name: "my-project" });
-await nm.writeFile("index.ts", "console.log('hello');");
-const result = await nm.exec("cat index.ts");
-// { exitCode: 0, stdout: "console.log('hello');", stderr: "" }
+```ts
+import { readFile, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";  // → R2 storage + SQLite cache
+import { exec } from "node:child_process";                // → DO built-in emulators
 ```
 
-## Module Shims — The vinext Approach
+## What the shims provide
 
-Like [vinext](https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext) shims `next/*` imports for Workers, nodemode shims `node:fs` and `node:child_process`:
+### `node:fs` → R2 + DO SQLite
 
-```jsonc
-// wrangler.jsonc
-{
-  "alias": {
-    "fs": "nodemode/shims/fs",
-    "child_process": "nodemode/shims/child_process"
-  }
-}
-```
+| fs method | Backing | Latency |
+|-----------|---------|---------|
+| `readFile` / `promises.readFile` | SQLite cache (<64KB) → R2 fallback | <1ms cached, 10-50ms R2 |
+| `writeFile` / `promises.writeFile` | R2 put + SQLite index + cache | ~10ms |
+| `stat` / `statSync` | SQLite lookup | <1ms |
+| `readdir` / `readdirSync` | SQLite prefix query | <1ms |
+| `mkdir` / `mkdirSync` | SQLite directory marker | <1ms |
+| `existsSync` | SQLite lookup | <1ms |
+| `unlink`, `rename`, `copyFile`, `rm`, `rmdir` | R2 + SQLite | ~10ms |
+| `appendFile`, `chmod`, `access`, `lstat` | R2 + SQLite | varies |
 
-Libraries that `import fs from "node:fs"` or `import { exec } from "node:child_process"` transparently get nodemode's R2+SQLite implementation — no Container, $0 cost.
+### `node:child_process` → DO ProcessManager
 
-| vinext | nodemode |
-|--------|----------|
-| Shims `next/link`, `next/router`, `next/image` | Shims `node:fs`, `node:child_process` |
-| Next.js apps on Workers | Node.js tools on Workers |
+| Method | What happens |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `exec(cmd, cb)` | Runs through ProcessManager — builtins in DO, rest to Container |
+| `spawn(cmd, args)` | Returns ChildProcess-like with stdout/stderr event emitters |
+| `execFile(file, args)` | Same as exec with args joined |
 
-## Architecture
+24 commands run entirely in the DO at $0:
+`echo`, `cat`, `ls`, `grep`, `head`, `tail`, `wc`, `mkdir`, `rm`, `cp`, `mv`, `touch`, `test`, `which`, `pwd`, `env`, `whoami`, `date`, `basename`, `dirname`, `printf`, `sleep`, `true`, `false`
 
-Commands are tiered:
-- **Built-in** (cat, ls, grep, head, tail, wc, echo, pwd, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, touch, test, which, basename, dirname, env, whoami, date, printf, sleep, true, false) — execute directly in DO, $0 cost, <1ms
-- **Container** (npm, node, git, tsc, esbuild, ...) — spawn Cloudflare Container, ~$0.02/hr
+Shell features: pipes (`|`), chains (`&&`, `||`, `;`), quoted strings.
 
-### Shell Support
+## Conformance tests
 
-- **Pipes**: `cmd1 | cmd2 | cmd3` — stdout of each stage feeds stdin of next
-- **Chains**: `&&` (stop on fail), `||` (run on fail), `;` (always run)
-- **Conditionals**: `test -f file && cat file || echo "not found"`
-- **Quoted strings**: single and double quotes preserved across pipes and chains
+235 tests verify the approach against patterns from popular tools (all run on builtins, no Container):
 
-### Container Integration
+| Tool | What it needs | Tests |
+|------|--------------|-------|
+| **opencode** (AI agent) | fs read/write, exec, refactoring, configs, monorepo | 61 |
+| **simple-git** | .git/ fs, refs, tags, remotes, merge conflicts, large files | 36 |
+| **zx** (Google) | pipes, chains, exit codes, conditionals, batch ops | 35 |
+| **create-next-app / create-vite / degit** | mkdir, write templates, npm install | 19 |
+| **lint-staged / nodemon / esbuild** | fs.watch, spawn linters, read imports, write bundles | 17 |
+| **Unit tests** | all fs ops, 24 builtins, shell parsing, validation | 67 |
 
-Non-builtin commands run inside a Cloudflare Container attached to the Workspace DO via `ctx.container`:
+## Architecture
 
-- **Boots on demand** — starts on first non-builtin exec, runs `container/agent.mjs` on port 8080
-- **R2 FUSE mount** — source files symlinked from `/mnt/workspace` into `/workspace`
-- **Local build artifacts** — `node_modules`, `dist`, `.npm`, `.next`, `build` on container-local disk
-- **Snapshot/restore** — SIGTERM triggers tar+zstd to R2, restored on next boot
-- **Inactivity timeout** — 10-minute idle sleep via `setInactivityTimeout()`
-- **Health checks** — DO alarm every 30s: `/healthz` ping + R2-to-SQLite index reconciliation
-- **Index invalidation** — container POSTs `/index-invalidate` when files change via R2 FUSE
+```
+Library does: import { readFile } from "node:fs"
+                         │
+                         ▼  (wrangler alias)
+              nodemode/shims/fs.ts
+                         │
+                         ▼
+              FsEngine (R2 + DO SQLite)
+              ├── SQLite cache hit (<1ms)
+              └── R2 fallback (10-50ms)
+
+Library does: import { exec } from "node:child_process"
+                         │
+                         ▼  (wrangler alias)
+              nodemode/shims/child_process.ts
+                         │
+                         ▼
+              ProcessManager
+              ├── Built-in? → execute in DO ($0, <1ms)
+              └── Not built-in? → route to Container (~$0.02/hr)
+```
 
-## Storage
+### Container (last resort)
 
-| Layer | Purpose | Limits |
-|-------|---------|--------|
-| **R2** | File content (`{workspace_id}/{path}`) | Unlimited |
-| **DO SQLite** | Directory index, file cache (<64KB), process table (last 1000, output truncated to 4KB), terminal buffer (last 5000 rows) | 1GB per DO |
-| **Container disk** | Build artifacts with R2 snapshot backup | Ephemeral |
+Commands not in the 24 built-ins (npm, node, git, tsc, python, etc.) route to a Cloudflare Container:
 
-## API
+- Boots on demand, sleeps after 10 min idle
+- R2 FUSE mount for source files
+- Snapshots `node_modules`/`dist` to R2 on shutdown, restores on boot
+- Health-checked every 30s by DO alarm
 
-All endpoints are under `/workspace/{id}/`:
+## Client SDK
 
-| Endpoint | Method | Description |
-|----------|--------|-------------|
-| `/init` | POST | Initialize workspace (owner, name) |
-| `/exec` | POST | Execute command (returns exitCode, stdout, stderr) |
-| `/fs/read` | POST | Read file (JSON or streaming) |
-| `/fs/write` | POST | Write file |
-| `/fs/stat` | GET | File metadata (size, mode, mtime, isDirectory) |
-| `/fs/readdir` | GET | List directory entries |
-| `/fs/mkdir` | POST | Create directory (supports recursive) |
-| `/fs/unlink` | POST | Delete file |
-| `/fs/rename` | POST | Rename/move file or directory |
-| `/fs/exists` | GET | Check if path exists |
-| `/process/list` | GET | List recent processes (metadata only) |
-| `/process/get` | GET | Get process details by pid (includes output) |
-| `/container/status` | GET | Container lifecycle status |
-| `/container/stop` | POST | Stop container (triggers snapshot) |
-| `/index-invalidate` | POST | Refresh SQLite index from R2 (used by container) |
+For interacting with nodemode over HTTP from any JavaScript environment:
 
-WebSocket upgrade on any path enables stdio streaming with terminal buffer replay.
+```ts
+import { NodeMode } from "nodemode/client";
 
-Workspace listing: `GET /api/workspaces` (paginated R2 prefix scan).
+const nm = new NodeMode("https://my-worker.workers.dev", "my-workspace");
+await nm.writeFile("index.ts", "console.log('hello');");
+const result = await nm.exec("cat index.ts | grep hello");
+```
 
-## Validation
+## Quick Start
 
-All inputs validated at system boundaries:
-- **Paths**: max 4096 chars, max 256 depth, no null bytes, no traversal above root
-- **Commands**: max 8192 chars, no null bytes, no empty
-- **Workspace IDs**: alphanumeric + `.`, `-`, `_`, max 128 chars
-- **Payloads**: max 100MB (Worker), max 10MB (container agent)
-- **Regex**: invalid grep patterns fall back to literal string matching
+```bash
+mkdir my-workspace && cd my-workspace
+npx nodemode init
+npm install
+npx wrangler dev   # local dev
+npx wrangler deploy # production
+```
 
 ## Running Tests
 
 ```bash
-# All tests (unit + conformance)
-npx vitest run
-
-# Unit tests only
-npx vitest run test/
-
-# Conformance tests only
-npx vitest run --dir research
+npx vitest run              # all tests (unit + conformance)
+npx vitest run test/        # unit tests only
+npx vitest run --dir research  # conformance tests only
 ```
 
 ## Known Gaps
 
 | Area | Status | Notes |
 |------|--------|-------|
-| Filesystem | ~85% | Streaming reads work. Missing: symlinks, file descriptors, R2+SQLite atomicity |
-| Shell parsing | ~50% | Pipes, chains, quoted strings work. Missing: redirects (`>`, `<`), variable expansion (`$VAR`), subshells, background jobs |
-| Container exec | ~70% | Exec + snapshot/restore working. Missing: streaming output, stdin piping, long-running processes |
-| Process model | ~40% | Process table with pruning (1000 rows). Missing: signals, stdin streaming, background processes |
-| Auth / security | ~20% | Path traversal protection, input validation, payload limits. Missing: authentication, rate limiting, per-workspace CORS |
-| Terminal buffer | ~70% | 5000-row buffer with trimming. Missing: per-stream limits, binary data |
-| Client SDK | ~60% | HTTP client works. Missing: WebSocket helpers, retry/reconnect, auth headers |
-| Workspace lifecycle | ~40% | Container lifecycle managed. Missing: workspace deletion, cloning, resource quotas |
-
-## Deploy
-
-```bash
-npx wrangler deploy
-```
-
-Requires Cloudflare Workers Paid plan (Durable Objects + R2).
+| fs shim | ~80% | Async API works. `readFileSync`/`writeFileSync` throw (async-only in DO). Missing: `watch`, `createReadStream`, symlinks |
+| child_process shim | ~60% | `exec`, `spawn`, `execFile` work. Missing: `fork`, stdin piping, real-time streaming |
+| Shell parsing | ~50% | Pipes, chains, quoted strings. Missing: redirects (`>`, `<`), `$VAR` expansion, subshells |
+| Auth / security | ~20% | Input validation, payload limits. Missing: authentication, rate limiting |
 
 ## License

         { label: "Child Process", slug: "child-process" },

@@ -1,15 +1,16 @@
 ---
 title: nodemode
-description: Node.js runtime on Cloudflare Workers — R2 filesystem, DO child_process, Container execution
+description: Experimental Node.js module shims for Cloudflare Workers
 ---
 
-**nodemode** maps Node.js primitives to Cloudflare primitives, giving you a serverless Node.js environment with real `fs`, `child_process`, and shell — things impossible on vanilla Cloudflare Workers.
+> **Experimental** — research prototype, not production-ready.
 
-## Why nodemode?
+**nodemode** is an experiment in making `node:fs` and `node:child_process` work on Cloudflare Workers by shimming them with R2 + DO SQLite — the same approach [vinext](https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext) uses to make `next/*` imports work on Workers.
 
-Cloudflare Workers have no `fs`, no `child_process`, and no shell. Web frameworks like Next.js work fine on Workers (via [vinext](https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext) or OpenNext), but dev tools, CLI scripts, build systems, and AI coding agents that need the filesystem and shell cannot run at all.
+## The problem
+
+Cloudflare Workers have no `fs`, no `child_process`, and no shell. Web frameworks work fine (vinext, OpenNext), but tools that need the filesystem and process spawning cannot run at all:
 
-nodemode bridges this gap:
 
 | Tool | What it needs | Workers alone | With nodemode |
 |------|--------------|---------------|---------------|
@@ -32,9 +33,9 @@ nodemode bridges this gap:
 | `stdin` / `stdout` / `stderr` | WebSocket + DO SQLite buffer | Working |
 | `process.env` | Worker env / Secrets | Working |
 
-## Conformance: 235 tests passing
+## Conformance tests
 
-Proves nodemode can support popular Node.js tools:
+235 tests verify the approach against patterns from popular tools:
 
 | Tool | Tests | Status |
 |------|-------|--------|

@@ -0,0 +1,196 @@
+---
+title: Module Shims
+description: Experimental node:fs and node:child_process shims for Cloudflare Workers
+---
+
+> **Experimental** — these shims cover common API surface but not the full Node.js spec. Many libraries will need testing.
+
+## Approach
+
+Inspired by [vinext](https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext) shimming `next/*` imports, nodemode experiments with shimming `node:fs` and `node:child_process` — replacing them with implementations backed by R2 and DO SQLite.
+
+## Setup
+
+Add module aliases to your `wrangler.jsonc`:
+
+```jsonc
+{
+  "alias": {
+    "fs": "nodemode/shims/fs",
+    "child_process": "nodemode/shims/child_process"
+  }
+}
+```
+
+That's it. Any library in your Worker that does `import fs from "node:fs"` or `require("fs")` now gets nodemode's implementation.
+
+## How it works
+
+```
+npm package does: import { readFile } from "node:fs"
+                           │
+                           ▼  wrangler alias resolves to
+                nodemode/shims/fs.ts
+                           │
+                           ▼  calls
+                FsEngine.readFile(path)
+                           │
+                    ┌──────┴──────┐
+                    ▼             ▼
+             SQLite cache     R2 bucket
+              (<1ms)          (10-50ms)
+```
+
+The shims bind to the active Workspace DO's `FsEngine` and `ProcessManager` via a context singleton. The `Workspace` DO calls `setContext()` on construction, so shims are ready before any user code runs.
+
+## fs shim API coverage
+
+### Async (callback) API
+
+```ts
+import fs from "node:fs";
+
+fs.readFile("config.json", "utf-8", (err, data) => { ... });
+fs.writeFile("output.txt", "content", (err) => { ... });
+fs.appendFile("log.txt", "new line\n", (err) => { ... });
+fs.stat("file.ts", (err, stats) => { console.log(stats.size); });
+fs.readdir("src", (err, files) => { ... });
+fs.mkdir("dist", { recursive: true }, (err) => { ... });
+fs.unlink("old.ts", (err) => { ... });
+fs.rename("a.ts", "b.ts", (err) => { ... });
+fs.copyFile("src.ts", "dest.ts", (err) => { ... });
+fs.rm("dir", { recursive: true }, (err) => { ... });
+fs.rmdir("dir", { recursive: true }, (err) => { ... });
+fs.chmod("file.ts", 0o755, (err) => { ... });
+fs.access("file.ts", (err) => { /* err if not found */ });
+fs.lstat("file.ts", (err, stats) => { ... }); // same as stat (no symlinks)
+```
+
+### Promises API
+
+```ts
+import { promises as fs } from "node:fs";
+// or: import fs from "node:fs/promises";
+
+const data = await fs.readFile("config.json", "utf-8");
+await fs.writeFile("output.txt", "content");
+await fs.appendFile("log.txt", "new line\n");
+const stats = await fs.stat("file.ts");
+const files = await fs.readdir("src");
+await fs.mkdir("dist", { recursive: true });
+await fs.unlink("old.ts");
+await fs.rename("a.ts", "b.ts");
+await fs.copyFile("src.ts", "dest.ts");
+await fs.rm("dir", { recursive: true, force: true });
+await fs.chmod("file.ts", 0o755);
+await fs.access("file.ts"); // throws if not found
+```
+
+### Sync API
+
+```ts
+import fs from "node:fs";
+
+fs.existsSync("file.ts");           // works — SQLite lookup
+fs.mkdirSync("dir", { recursive: true });  // works — SQLite insert
+fs.readdirSync("src");              // works — SQLite query
+fs.statSync("file.ts");             // works — SQLite lookup
+fs.lstatSync("file.ts");            // works — same as statSync
+```
+
+`readFileSync` and `writeFileSync` throw — R2 is async-only. Use `fs.promises` instead.
+
+### Stats object
+
+The `Stats` object returned by `stat`/`statSync` supports:
+
+```ts
+stats.size        // file size in bytes
+stats.mode        // file permissions
+stats.mtime       // Date object
+stats.mtimeMs     // milliseconds
+stats.isFile()    // true if not directory
+stats.isDirectory() // true if directory
+stats.isSymbolicLink() // always false
+```
+
+## child_process shim API coverage
+
+### exec
+
+```ts
+import { exec } from "node:child_process";
+
+exec("cat file.txt | grep pattern", (err, stdout, stderr) => {
+  console.log(stdout); // matched lines
+});
+
+exec("ls -la", { cwd: "/project", env: { NODE_ENV: "test" } }, (err, stdout) => {
+  console.log(stdout);
+});
+```
+
+### spawn
+
+```ts
+import { spawn } from "node:child_process";
+
+const child = spawn("grep", ["ERROR", "log.txt"]);
+
+child.stdout.on("data", (data) => {
+  console.log("stdout:", data);
+});
+
+child.stderr.on("data", (data) => {
+  console.error("stderr:", data);
+});
+
+child.on("close", (code) => {
+  console.log("exit code:", code);
+});
+```
+
+### execFile
+
+```ts
+import { execFile } from "node:child_process";
+
+execFile("cat", ["file1.txt", "file2.txt"], (err, stdout) => {
+  console.log(stdout);
+});
+```
+
+### What runs where
+
+| Command | Where | Cost | Latency |
+|---------|-------|------|---------|
+| `echo`, `cat`, `ls`, `grep`, `head`, `tail`, `wc`, `mkdir`, `rm`, `cp`, `mv`, `touch`, `test`, `which`, `pwd`, `env`, `whoami`, `date`, `basename`, `dirname`, `printf`, `sleep`, `true`, `false` | DO (built-in) | $0 | <1ms |
+| `npm`, `node`, `git`, `tsc`, `esbuild`, `python`, `cargo`, etc. | Container | ~$0.02/hr | seconds |
+
+## Not supported
+
+| API | Why |
+|-----|-----|
+| `fs.readFileSync` / `writeFileSync` | R2 is async-only. Use `fs.promises` |
+| `fs.watch` / `watchFile` | No file watching in DO |
+| `fs.createReadStream` / `createWriteStream` | Use `readFile`/`writeFile` or the REST streaming API |
+| `child_process.fork` | Workers are single-threaded |
+| `child_process.execSync` | DO execution is async |
+| Symlinks | R2 has no symlink concept |
+
+## Custom context
+
+If you're using nodemode outside the built-in `Workspace` DO, bind the context manually:
+
+```ts
+import { setContext, clearContext } from "nodemode";
+
+// In your DO constructor or request handler:
+setContext(myFsEngine, myProcessManager);
+
+// Run your code that uses node:fs / node:child_process shims
+await runUserCode();
+
+// Optional cleanup
+clearContext();
+```
